Summary:

The paper discusses various machine learning-based approaches for detecting and classifying malware. The authors examine different techniques, including image processing, convolutional neural networks (CNNs), and recurrent neural networks (RNNs), to identify and categorize malware. The study highlights the limitations of signature-based techniques and the importance of using machine learning algorithms for malware detection. The authors also discuss the concept of variant malicious code detection and its application in identifying novel variations of malware.

Categorization:

I would categorize this paper as "Demonstrated." The paper presents various machine learning-based approaches for detecting and classifying malware, including image processing, CNNs, and RNNs. The authors demonstrate the effectiveness of these techniques by applying them to different datasets and comparing their performance. The paper also discusses the limitations and challenges of each approach, providing a comprehensive overview of the current state-of-the-art in malware detection.
